description: Add a 'Restore approvals' link to the skip-permissions banner in the side panel. Clicking it sends a request to the MCP server to set state.skipPermissions = false. The banner disappears, and permission resolution resumes normal behavior. This is a one-way action — there is no UI to re-enable skip-permissions.

userStories.title: Add config.setSkipPermissions server-side handler
userStories.description: Add a new JSON-RPC method 'config.setSkipPermissions' to the extension protocol that sets state.skipPermissions to the provided boolean value. Follow the exact same pattern as config.setPluginPermission. After updating state, send a plugins.changed notification so the extension cache and side panel update.
acceptanceCriteria: config.setSkipPermissions with { skipPermissions: false } sets state.skipPermissions to false
A plugins.changed notification is sent after updating state (so extension cache refreshes)
A { result: { ok: true } } response is returned to the caller
The method is dispatched from handleExtensionMessage in extension-protocol.ts

userStories.title: Add bg:setSkipPermissions background message handler
userStories.description: Add a new background message handler that the side panel can call to send config.setSkipPermissions to the MCP server. Follow the same optimistic-update pattern as bg:setPluginPermission.
acceptanceCriteria: Side panel can call sendBgMessage({ type: 'bg:setSkipPermissions', skipPermissions: false })
Background handler sends config.setSkipPermissions request to server via sendServerRequest
On failure, the cache is rolled back to the original skipPermissions value

userStories.title: Add setSkipPermissions bridge function and 'Restore approvals' link in side panel
userStories.description: Add a bridge function for the new message type, and add a 'Restore approvals' text link to the skip-permissions banner in App.tsx. Clicking the link calls the bridge function with skipPermissions: false. On success, the banner disappears (state updates via the existing getFullState refresh triggered by plugins.changed).
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Restore approvals' link appears on its own line below the banner's description text
Clicking the link calls setSkipPermissions(false) and the banner disappears
The link is styled as font-head text-destructive text-[11px] underline cursor-pointer — matching the banner's scale
The bridge function setSkipPermissions is exported from bridge.ts
No button chrome — this is a text link, not a retro Button component
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (add setSkipPermissions function) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (add link to banner) Context: The banner currently renders at App.tsx lines 348-359: ```tsx {skipPermissions && (
Approvals skipped

AI runs tools without asking. Off tools stay off. Set by{' '} OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS.

)} ``` The side panel state is: `const [skipPermissions, setSkipPermissions] = useState(false);` (line 44). It is set from `applyFullState` (line 217): `setSkipPermissions(result.skipPermissions ?? false);`. When the server sends plugins.changed after our config.setSkipPermissions call, the side panel calls `getFullState()` → `applyFullState()` which sets `skipPermissions` to `false`, and the banner disappears. Changes: 1. In bridge.ts, add a new function near the other setter functions (around line 125): ```ts const setSkipPermissions = (skipPermissions: boolean): Promise => sendBgMessage({ type: 'bg:setSkipPermissions', skipPermissions }); ``` Add it to the exports at the bottom of the file. 2. In App.tsx, add the restore link as a new line below the

in the banner. Use a )} ``` The `void` is needed because bridge.setSkipPermissions returns a Promise and the onClick handler should not return it. We don't need to handle errors — the existing plugins.changed → getFullState flow handles the state update. If the request fails, the banner stays (which is correct — the server is still in skip mode). Constraints: - Do NOT use the retro Button component — this is a lightweight text link, not a full button - Do NOT add a confirmation dialog — the action is non-destructive (restoring safety) and one-way - Do NOT add local state for loading/disabled — if the user clicks and it fails, the banner stays visible and they can click again. Keep it simple. - The link text is 'Restore approvals' (not 'Disable' or 'Turn off' — positive framing)
